consistency.class_match: Consistent. The feature belongs to the 'druid' class.
consistency.level_match: Consistent. Level 1 is standard for many core class features.

quality.clarity: High. The description clearly states the prerequisite (spell has the ritual tag) and the effect (can cast as a ritual if prepared).
quality.completeness: Sufficient. It covers the necessary mechanical scope for a ritual casting ability.

mechanics.type: Core Class Feature (Implicitly, as it grants a universal ability)
prerequisites: Spell must be prepared by the caster.
mechanics.effect: Allows the casting of ritual spells without expending a spell slot.
mechanics.notes: This mirrors the standard Player's Handbook mechanic for classes that gain the Ritual Casting feature (like Wizard or Cleric/Druid in some interpretations), granting the ability to use the ritual tag mechanic.

analysis.suggestions: No mandatory changes suggested. This feature perfectly encapsulates the standard D&D 5e mechanic for granting ritual casting to a class that might not inherently have it or formalizes it at level 1.
